sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: PG&E appoints Chelle Izzi as new chief commercial officer
feed.summary: PG&E has appointed Chelle Izzi as its new Chief Commercial Officer to lead the company's commercial strategy, particularly focusing on serving large electric customers such as AI-driven data centers. Izzi brings over 25 years of experience in the clean energy sector, and her role is crucial as PG&E anticipates electricity demand to roughly double by 2040. The company also reported mixed Q3 2025 financial results, exceeding EPS expectations but falling short on revenue.

feed.title: Golden properties LTD sells $1.58 million in American resources (AREC)
feed.summary: Golden Properties LTD, a significant shareholder in American Resources Corp (AREC), sold 395,045 shares of Class A Common Stock for approximately $1.58 million on November 7, 2025. This transaction reduced Golden Properties LTD.'s stake to 10,834,940 shares. The article also highlights American Resources Corporation's recent capital-raising efforts, including a $1.4 billion partnership for rare earth magnet production and private placements totaling over $70 million to develop critical mineral processing hubs.

feed.title: Sotera Health (SHC) chairman Petras sells shares worth $12.3m
feed.summary: Michael B. Petras Jr., Chairman and CEO of Sotera Health Co (NASDAQ:SHC), sold 749,199 shares of common stock for approximately $12.3 million on December 8, 2025. These sales were executed at $16.45 per share, slightly below the current trading price. The company has a market capitalization of $4.75 billion and its shares have surged 35.85% over the past six months, currently trading near their 52-week high.

feed.title: United Rentals, Inc. $URI Shares Sold by The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company
feed.summary: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company decreased its stake in United Rentals, Inc. by 5.3% during Q2, selling 45,035 shares and now holding 804,751 shares valued at $606.3 million. Despite this, institutional ownership remains high at 96.26%, with other institutions like Norges Bank and JPMorgan Chase & Co. increasing their holdings. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for United Rentals, with an average target price of $968.94.

feed.title: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company Sells 113,958 Shares of Texas Instruments Incorporated $TXN
feed.summary: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company reduced its stake in Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN) by 3.4% during the second quarter, selling 113,958 shares and now owning 3,240,855 shares valued at approximately $672.9 million. This adjustment occurred as Texas Instruments reported solid earnings with $1.48 EPS, revenue of $4.74 billion, and increased its quarterly dividend to $1.42. Institutional investors collectively own 84.99% of the company's stock, even as some insiders have recently sold shares.

feed.title: WINTON GROUP Ltd Purchases New Stake in ONEOK, Inc. $OKE
feed.summary: WINTON GROUP Ltd has acquired a new position in ONEOK, Inc. (NYSE:OKE), purchasing 14,271 shares valued at approximately $1,165,000 during the second quarter. This move is part of broader institutional investor activity, with several other firms also increasing their stakes in the utilities provider. The article also provides a financial overview of ONEOK, including its stock performance, recent earnings, dividend announcement, and analyst ratings.

feed.title: MIT CLIMATE MACHINE WITH SUPPORT FROM COLDPLAY, WARNER MUSIC GROUP, LIVE NATION, AND HOPE SOLUTIONS RELEASE FIRST EVER TOTAL EMISSIONS OF LIVE MUSIC IN US AND UK
feed.summary: The MIT Climate Machine, supported by major music industry players, has released the first comprehensive study on the carbon emissions of the live music industry in the US and UK. Analyzing over 80,000 events, the report identifies fan travel as the largest emissions driver, followed by food and beverage consumption. The findings aim to provide a foundation for decarbonizing live entertainment and inspire broader climate action within the sector.

feed.title: WINTON GROUP Ltd Buys New Shares in KKR & Co. Inc. $KKR
feed.summary: WINTON GROUP Ltd has purchased a new position of 7,788 shares in KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE:KKR) during the second quarter, valued at approximately $1,036,000. Other institutional investors like Southeast Asset Advisors LLC and Bruce G. Allen Investments LLC also increased their stakes in the asset manager. KKR & Co. Inc. recently reported strong quarterly earnings, topping analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Moss for Promarket: Canceling the Antitrust Show? Live Nation-Ticketmaster’s Latest Attempt To Keep Its Monopoly
feed.summary: Live Nation-Ticketmaster has filed a motion for summary judgment in its antitrust lawsuit, attempting to dismiss the case brought by the DOJ and 40 state attorneys general. The lawsuit alleges that the company uses its monopoly power in ticketing, concert promotion, and venue management to stifle competition, harming consumers, rival promoters, and artists. If successful, the case could lead to breaking up Ticketmaster from Live Nation, providing significant relief to music fans.

feed.title: New Intuit AI Suites Promise Faster Deployment and ROI
feed.summary: Intuit has launched new AI-native solutions, including Intuit Accountant Suite and enhancements to Intuit Enterprise Suite, to significantly improve financial management for businesses and accounting firms. These innovations, unveiled by CEO Sasan Goodzar, aim to streamline operations, provide data-backed insights, and automate tasks through embedded AI agents, ultimately leading to faster deployment, higher ROI, and a competitive advantage in the new era. The platform is designed to unify business data, saving users up to 12 hours per month, and has shown nearly a 300% return on investment in a Forrester study.

feed.title: EQT Completes Full Exit from Kodiak Gas Services Investment
feed.summary: EQT Corp has fully divested its interests in Kodiak Gas Services, marking its first IPO from the infrastructure portfolio after a seven-year collaboration. The natural gas production company, focusing on Marcellus and Utica shales, maintains a strong financial position despite recent insider selling. EQT's valuation metrics are near one-year lows, and analyst sentiment remains positive, with institutional ownership reflecting strong confidence.

feed.title: Denver Broncos and Verizon Business enhance stadium operations and fan experiences
feed.summary: The Denver Broncos and Verizon Business have significantly upgraded the fan experience at Empower Field at Mile High through a new Wi-Fi 6E and Local Area Network installation. This network refresh includes over 2,400 wireless access points, enhancing connectivity for fans and improving stadium operations for staff. These enhancements ensure fast, reliable internet access for devices and streamline systems ranging from POS to digital ticketing, building on previous 5G upgrades.

feed.title: The Walt Disney Company and OpenAI Reach Agreement to Bring Disney Characters to Sora
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company and OpenAI have struck a three-year licensing deal allowing Sora to generate short, user-prompted social videos featuring over 200 Disney, Marvel, Pixar, and Star Wars characters. This agreement enables Disney fans to create and share content, with curated selections available on Disney+. Concurrently, Disney will invest $1 billion in OpenAI, become a major customer using its APIs for new products, and deploy ChatGPT internally, all while affirming a commitment to responsible AI use.

feed.title: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company Purchases 88,423 Shares of Stryker Corporation $SYK
feed.summary: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company increased its stake in Stryker Corporation by 7.5% in Q2, acquiring 88,423 shares to hold a total of 1,271,247 shares valued at approximately $502.9 million. Institutional investors now own about 77.09% of Stryker's stock, with a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and a $433.78 average target price. This comes despite recent insider selling, which totaled 522,118 shares in the past three months.

feed.title: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company Trims Stake in Philip Morris International Inc. $PM
feed.summary: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company decreased its holding in Philip Morris International Inc. by 1.1% in Q2, divesting 21,815 shares, yet still maintaining 1,974,016 shares valued at $359.53 million. Analysts rate Philip Morris International as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $189.00, and the company recently increased its quarterly dividend to $1.47, resulting in a 3.9% yield.

feed.title: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company Increases Stock Holdings in Medtronic PLC $MDT
feed.summary: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company significantly increased its stake in Medtronic PLC by 7.8% in Q2, acquiring 445,174 additional shares to hold a total of 6,155,810 shares, valued at approximately $536.6 million. Medtronic also reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates with EPS of $1.36 and revenue of $8.96 billion, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.71 per share. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average price target of $110.44.

feed.title: L3Harris secures $200m contract for GMLRS IM propulsion units
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies has won a follow-on production contract worth up to $200m from Lockheed Martin to supply Guided Multiple Launch Rocket System (GMLRS) Insensitive Munition (IM) propulsion units. This contract represents the largest GMLRS IM order L3Harris has received from Lockheed Martin within a single 12-month period, and the work will be executed at L3Harris' newly built facility in Camden, Arkansas, which will increase annual production capacity by over 30%. The company's output of GMLRS propulsion units has already risen by almost 80% due to new manufacturing methods.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc. Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc. (NASDAQ: SFM) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between June 4, 2025, and October 29, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Sprouts made materially false and misleading statements regarding its growth potential and the resilience of its customer base to macroeconomic pressures. Investors are encouraged to contact the firm to discuss their legal rights.

feed.title: EOG Resources (NYSE: EOG) adds former Williams and Magellan CFO John D. Chandler to board
feed.summary: EOG Resources (NYSE: EOG) has appointed John D. Chandler, a veteran energy finance executive, to its Board of Directors, effective December 10, 2025. Chandler previously served as CFO for Williams and Magellan Midstream Partners, bringing extensive financial expertise and experience in crude oil and natural gas midstream transportation. His appointment is expected to be a valuable asset to EOG's board.

feed.title: Leandra Medine Cohen Sells Her Closet on eBay
feed.summary: Leandra Medine Cohen is selling over 30 hand-picked pieces from her personal archive, including items from Celine, Prada, and Maison Margiela, exclusively on eBay. The auction, part of eBay's "From the Collection" series, started with bidding at $0.99 and continues through December 18. Proceeds from the sale will benefit Sharsheret, a nonprofit supporting Jewish women and families affected by breast and ovarian cancer.

feed.title: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company Acquires 469,008 Shares of Danaher Corporation $DHR
feed.summary: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company increased its stake in Danaher Corporation by 24.6% in Q2, acquiring 469,008 additional shares to hold a total of 2,376,853 shares valued at approximately $469.5 million. Danaher recently beat Q2 earnings estimates with $1.89 EPS and $6.05 billion in revenue. Analysts currently rate DHR as a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $246.79.

feed.title: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company Lowers Position in Bank Of Montreal $BMO
feed.summary: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company significantly reduced its stake in Bank of Montreal (NYSE:BMO) by 44.9% in the second quarter, selling over 2.7 million shares and retaining approximately 3.3 million shares valued at $369.3 million. Despite this reduction, Bank of Montreal reported strong Q2 earnings, beating analyst estimates for both EPS and revenue, and announced an increased quarterly dividend of $1.67 per share, yielding 5.2%. The stock currently holds a "Hold" consensus rating with a target price of $163.00 from MarketBeat analysts.

feed.title: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company Sells 2,665,536 Shares of Crown Castle Inc. $CCI
feed.summary: The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company significantly reduced its stake in Crown Castle Inc. (NYSE:CCI) by selling over 2.6 million shares, decreasing its total holding to 0.77% of the company. However, other institutional investors like Vanguard Group and State Street Corp increased their positions in Crown Castle. The company recently announced a quarterly dividend of $1.0625 per share and reported Q2 EPS of $1.12, exceeding expectations.

feed.title: Verizon (NYSE: VZ) deploys 2,400 Wi‑Fi 6E access points at Broncos’ Empower Field
feed.summary: Verizon Business has upgraded Empower Field at Mile High by deploying over 2,400 Wi-Fi 6E access points and a dedicated 6GHz frequency backbone, enhancing fan experience and stadium operations. This network refresh replaces the legacy Wi-Fi system and includes WPA3 encryption for improved security. Verizon had previously installed 965 antennas for 5G coverage, integrating these latest Wi-Fi advancements into its broader Connected Venues strategy.

feed.title: MMTEC, Inc. Secures Nasdaq Compliance Amid Bid Price Concerns
feed.summary: MMTEC, Inc. has secured Nasdaq compliance following concerns over its minimum bid price. The company received a decision letter from the Nasdaq Hearings Panel affirming its compliance after a pre-hearing submission showed improved stock performance, averting delisting. MMTEC was placed under a one-year monitoring period and maintains a Hold rating with a $3.00 price target from the most recent analyst.

feed.title: SVB Wealth LLC Sells 17,762 Shares of Corteva, Inc. $CTVA
feed.summary: SVB Wealth LLC has reduced its stake in Corteva, Inc. by 85.8%, selling 17,762 shares and now owning 2,931 shares worth $218,000. Other institutional investors have both increased and decreased their positions in Corteva, with 81.54% of the stock owned by hedge funds and institutions. The company recently reported better-than-expected earnings and announced a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Baxter reports safety improvements with smart infusion pump integration
feed.summary: Baxter International Inc. announced data demonstrating improved patient safety and clinical workflow efficiency through the integration of smart infusion pumps with hospital electronic medical records. A study conducted with The University of Texas Medical Branch revealed significant reductions in safety alerts and faster infusion programming times after EMR integration. Despite these advancements, Baxter faces financial challenges, though analysts anticipate a return to profitability this year.

feed.title: Ala. FD to equip firefighters, medics and inspectors with body-worn cameras
feed.summary: The Birmingham City Council has approved a three-year, $170,000 agreement with Axon for body-worn cameras for its Fire and Rescue Services. These cameras will be utilized by firefighters, paramedics, and fire inspectors primarily for training purposes and to aid in incident investigations. City officials anticipate this technology will significantly improve the visual documentation and understanding of incidents, similar to how police body cameras function.

feed.title: Medtronic studies highlight MiniMed 780G’s cost-effectiveness
feed.summary: Medtronic announced new data showcasing the health economic benefits of its MiniMed 780G system. Studies conducted in Japan, Hong Kong, and Australia demonstrated that the MiniMed 780G consistently improved quality of life and reduced long-term risks of diabetes-related complications, leading to meaningful cost savings that partially offset the system's upfront expenses. These findings were presented at ATTD Asia 2025, emphasizing the growing value of automated insulin delivery systems for managing type 1 diabetes.

feed.title: SLB partners with Shell to develop AI solutions for energy operations
feed.summary: SLB (NYSE:SLB) has announced a strategic collaboration with Shell to develop digital and AI solutions for upstream oil and gas operations. This partnership will focus on agentic AI-powered solutions to enhance decision-making and efficiency, utilizing SLB’s Lumi data and AI platform for unified data and workflows. The agreement builds on an existing relationship and aims to create broadly applicable, commercially viable digital solutions for the energy industry.

feed.title: Cantor Fitzgerald Adjusts PT on Willis Towers Watson Public to $365 From $355, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Cantor Fitzgerald has increased its price target for Willis Towers Watson Public (WTW) to $365 from $355, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst recent financial news for the company, including acquisitions and dividend approvals. The firm also reported its quarterly dividend will be $0.92 per share, payable on January 15, 2026.

feed.title: Boston Scientific announces positive coverage policy for Intracept procedure
feed.summary: Boston Scientific announced that Health Care Service Corporation (HCSC), a major U.S. commercial health insurer, now covers the Intracept Procedure for eligible patients with chronic vertebrogenic low back pain. This policy, effective December 1, 2025, extends coverage to over 26 million people nationwide, acknowledging the clinical value and expanding access to this FDA-cleared, minimally invasive treatment. The Intracept Procedure is described as the only therapy specifically designed to target vertebrogenic pain with proven safety, effectiveness, and durability through long-term clinical studies.

feed.title: My Most Contrarian Investment For 2026: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR)
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment (CZR) is presented as a high-risk, high-reward investment for 2026, despite an 80% decline from its 2021 highs due to recession fears. The article argues that the market is underestimating the growth of its digital business and the value of its real estate, suggesting a fair value three times higher than its current price. This contrarian view is offered as an opportunity with significant upside potential.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts Price Target on Halliburton to $33 From $31, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has revised its price target for Halliburton (HAL) shares to $33, up from $31, while continuing to recommend a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects Citigroup's updated outlook on the oil-related services and equipment company. Halliburton's stock was trading at $29.04, showing a slight increase in pre-market activity.

feed.title: Xcel Energy (XEL) Implements Oracle Opower to Enhance Customer E
feed.summary: Xcel Energy has partnered with Oracle to integrate its AI-driven Opower platform, aiming to enhance customer engagement and grid resilience. While this strategic move advances energy initiatives and offers customers tailored consumption insights, the company faces financial vulnerabilities including a low Altman Z-Score and high debt levels. Valuation metrics suggest potential overvaluation, advising caution despite the promising technological partnership.

feed.title: Walleye Capital LLC Purchases Shares of 46,604 PDF Solutions, Inc. $PDFS
feed.summary: Walleye Capital LLC has acquired a new stake of 46,604 shares in PDF Solutions (NASDAQ:PDFS), valued at approximately $996,000, representing 0.12% ownership as of Q2. Institutional investors now hold 79.51% of PDFS, with several firms recently increasing or initiating positions. Analysts currently rate PDFS as a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of $38.00.

feed.title: Jacobs Solutions Inc. $J Stock Holdings Trimmed by WINTON GROUP Ltd
feed.summary: WINTON GROUP Ltd significantly reduced its stake in Jacobs Solutions Inc. (NYSE:J) by 82.0%, selling 43,172 shares, although other major institutional investors increased their holdings. Company insiders, including Directors Peter J. Robertson and Manuel J. Fernandez, recently purchased shares, accumulating 2,513 shares in the last three months. Jacobs Solutions reported mixed quarterly earnings with EPS beating consensus but revenue missing estimates, and it has a "Moderate Buy" consensus analyst rating with a $155.90 price target.

feed.title: EPAM Systems warns of urgent need for AI governance
feed.summary: EPAM Systems emphasizes the crucial need for responsible AI governance, particularly with the rapid growth of generative AI. The company highlights the transformational impact of AI on organizations and value delivery, advocating for robust ethical frameworks to manage these innovations effectively. This stance aligns with EPAM's broader efforts in technological innovation, including using AI to cut downtime and strengthening fraud prevention for financial institutions.

feed.title: Saratoga Investment Corp. Declares $0.75 Quarterly Dividend for Q4 Fiscal 2026
feed.summary: Saratoga Investment Corp. (NYSE:SAR) announced a base quarterly dividend of $0.75 per share for Q4 fiscal 2026, payable monthly in $0.25 installments from December 2025 to February 2026. This dividend implies an annualized yield of 13.1% based on a recent stock price of $22.86. The company emphasizes its commitment to consistent shareholder returns, offering shareholders the option to receive dividends in cash or through a dividend reinvestment plan.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank lifts J.B. Hunt rating to 'buy', hikes PT
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has upgraded J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) to "buy" from "hold" and significantly increased its price target to $227 from $133, citing the company's strong position to benefit from rising truck rates through 2026. The brokerage anticipates J.B. Hunt's earnings growth will surpass the market's over the next two years, driven by a shift towards more cost-efficient rail and intermodal transport. This positive outlook is echoed by other analysts, with 14 out of 25 brokerages rating JBHT "buy" or higher.

feed.title: Rokos Capital Management LLP Has $84.22 Million Holdings in Huntington Ingalls Industries, Inc. $HII
feed.summary: Rokos Capital Management LLP reduced its stake in Huntington Ingalls Industries, Inc. (NYSE:HII) by 9.6% in the second quarter, yet the stock remains its 18th largest holding, valued at $84.22 million. Other institutional investors have also adjusted their positions, and analysts have set new price targets for the aerospace company. Insider selling activity was also noted, with key executives selling shares.

feed.title: SLB Enters Collaboration Agreement to Accelerate New Digital Solutions
feed.summary: SLB has signed a strategic collaboration agreement with Shell to develop digital and AI solutions aimed at improving performance and efficiency in upstream operations. This partnership will leverage SLB’s Lumi™ data and AI platform to create agentic AI-powered solutions that integrate data and workflows across subsurface, well construction, and production, facilitating automation and autonomy throughout the industry. The collaboration builds on an existing relationship, including the earlier deployment of SLB's Petrel™ subsurface software across Shell’s assets.

feed.title: German American Bancorp Inc. Raises Stock Position in RTX Corporation $RTX
feed.summary: German American Bancorp Inc. significantly increased its stake in RTX Corporation by 107.3% during the 2nd quarter, now holding 62,883 shares valued at $9.18 million. Other institutional investors like Norges Bank and Vanguard Group Inc. have also made substantial investments in RTX, contributing to 86.50% institutional ownership. RTX recently announced a quarterly dividend of $0.68 and reported strong Q3 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin (LMT) Unveils New AI Initiative for Government Applications
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin's subsidiary, AstrisAI, has launched the "Astris AI for Government" initiative to enhance secure and reliable AI applications for government use, aligning with the U.S. AI Action Plan. Despite strong financial health indicated by an Altman Z-Score of 3.13, the company faces challenges such as declining margins and high debt levels. Valuation metrics offer a mixed picture, with a P/E ratio near its 5-year high and a P/S ratio near a 10-year low.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) Downgraded by Argus Research | CL Stock N
feed.summary: Argus Research has downgraded Colgate-Palmolive (CL) from Buy to Hold, with analyst Taylor Conrad making the decision without disclosing a new price target. This downgrade comes amidst varying analyst ratings, including a recent upgrade by RBC Capital to Outperform and several price target adjustments by other firms. Despite the mixed signals, the average target price from 21 analysts suggests an 11.98% upside from the current price for the consumer packaged goods giant.

feed.title: T-Mobile Marks $20M Milestone in Hometown Grants, Reaching 450 Communities
feed.summary: T-Mobile has announced the latest 25 recipients of its Hometown Grants program, bringing the total investment to over $20 million across 450 small towns and rural communities in 48 states and Puerto Rico since April 2021. These grants, up to $50,000 per town, fund projects that strengthen communities, such as libraries, parks, and technology upgrades, leading to 179,000 volunteer hours and over 2,000 jobs created. T-Mobile partners with Main Street America to select recipients, focusing on proposals with significant community impact and feasibility.

feed.title: Stifel reiterates Hold rating on Federal Realty Investment Trust stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Stifel maintained its Hold rating and $104.50 price target for Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT). The company is progressing on several redevelopment projects and has a strong dividend payment history, but its P/E ratio is high relative to near-term earnings growth. Recent Q3 2025 earnings showed mixed performance with an EPS miss but revenue beat.

feed.title: November's Infrastructure Winner: The $43B Company Beating Industry Titans on Every Metric
feed.summary: Martin Marietta Materials Inc. is identified as November's top infrastructure stock, leading in operational excellence with superior operating and profit margins compared to competitors. The article analyzes the Q3 2025 financial performance of five leading infrastructure companies, including Caterpillar, United Rentals, EMCOR Group, and Vulcan Materials, highlighting their revenue growth, profitability, and strategic positioning. Despite mixed signals across the sector, Martin Marietta stands out due to its industry-leading profitability and strong market position.

feed.title: Stryker raises quarterly dividend by 4.8% to $0.88 per share
feed.summary: Medical technology company Stryker (NYSE:SYK) has announced a 4.8% increase in its quarterly dividend, raising it to $0.88 per share, payable on January 30, 2026. This decision reflects the company's confidence in its financial performance and capital allocation priorities. The announcement follows other recent positive developments for Stryker, including a new President and COO appointment and reiterated "Outperform" ratings from multiple analyst firms.

feed.title: AI chip and tech stocks are falling again after gloomy Oracle earnings reignite bubble fears. Here's the latest
feed.summary: AI chip and tech stocks are falling after Oracle reported disappointing Q2 2026 earnings, renewing fears of an AI bubble fueled by "circular spending." Despite Oracle's non-GAAP EPS beating expectations, its total revenue fell short, causing investors to question the sustainability of current AI investments. This slump is impacting major tech companies and chip makers, with Oracle's stock alone down over 12%.
